problem text: A fair $6$-sided die is rolled repeatedly until the total of all rolls reaches or exceeds $1000$. Which number on the die is most likely to appear on the final roll?
problem solution: This is because $6$ can achieve $1000$ from any total between $994-999$. In contrast, a $1$ is only feasible if the cumulative total is $999$. A smaller number on the final roll limits the possible sums prior to surpassing $1000$.
problem hint: For what current totals can $1000$ be achieved if the die rolls an $i$, where $1 \leq i \leq 6$ on the last roll?
